commands.review_summary:
This PR changes Anthropic Claude CLI backend normalization so `bypassPermissions` is only added when OpenClaw exec policy resolves to full access with asks disabled, and adjusts related tests plus local gate-drift files.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +35, Other +1. Total +72 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: with the PR head, `resolveCliBackendConfig("claude-cli")` passes a context without config, the new `!exec` branch returns no mode, and current main tests expect default `bypassPermissions`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide the default Claude CLI permission contract and require a clean branch plus real behavior proof before merge.

target.title: fix(anthropic): stop default Claude permission bypass
target.body:
## Summary

- Stop defaulting Claude CLI runs to `--permission-mode bypassPermissions`; OpenClaw now only requests that mode when the explicit exec policy is full access with asks disabled.
- Preserve upstream Claude CLI safety defaults from `main`, including `--allowedTools mcp__openclaw__*` and user setting sources, while keeping user-provided valid permission modes intact.
- Repair merged-main local gate drift in stale session lock handling, async attachment cleanup tests, embedded runner harness registration, TypeBox mocks, provider auth contract expectations, and bundled canvas metadata.

## Verification

Commands run:
- `pnpm test:extension anthropic`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/sandbox/registry.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-misc.config.ts extensions/llm-task/src/llm-task-tool.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-provider-openai.config.ts extensions/openai/provider-auth.contract.test.ts extensions/openai/provider-catalog.contract.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/bundled-plugin-metadata.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts`
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm test`
- `pnpm build`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Claude CLI invocations no longer receive a default permission bypass unless OpenClaw explicitly requested YOLO/full-access execution.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout after merging current `origin/main`.

Observed result after fix: Anthropic extension tests cover omission of permission flags by default, removal of legacy default bypass, preservation of explicit split/equals permission modes, dropping malformed permission-mode inputs, and explicit YOLO-only bypass. Full local check, test, and build gates all pass.
